# Tamper matrix packs (step 7)

Generated by `node tools/gen-fixtures.mjs` - an independent Node reference
implementation of the frozen rules (JCS entry rendering, 0x00/0x01 Merkle
domain separation). Do not edit by hand: CI regenerates the tree and fails
on any byte drift (fixture rot guard). The whole subtree is `-text` in
`.gitattributes`; payload and manifest bytes are digest-sensitive.

`tools/cli-test.ps1` runs `verify --json` on every pack and asserts the
**exact** finding-code multiset plus the exit code (no "at least contains"
assertions).

| Pack | Tamper | Exit | Exact codes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| valid | none (baseline) | 0 | - |
| tampered-file | payload byte flip, manifest untouched | 1 | E2003 |
| missing-file | listed file absent on disk | 1 | E2003 |
| unlisted-file | extra payload not in manifest | 0 | W1001 |
| bad-digest-field | manifest digest field forged after sealing | 1 | E2003, E3003 |
| bad-merkle-root | root field swapped for wrong digest | 1 | E3003 |
| chain-broken | parent id never existed | 1 | E4002 |
| chain-cycle | rootless two-node cycle | 1 | E4003 |
| chain-empty | empty version chain array | 1 | E4001 |
| chain-fork | two children share one parent | 1 | E4004 |

Semantics worth noting (asserted by the matrix):

- `tampered-file` fires **only** E2003: the Merkle tree attests manifest
  entries, not file bytes, so the root still matches the (stale) entries.
- `bad-digest-field` fires **both** E2003 and E3003: the forged digest
  field disagrees with the file *and* changes the leaf bytes under the
  sealed root - each defense line catches its own aspect.
- `unlisted-file` is a warning: verification passes (exit 0) but W1001
  flags the unattested payload.

Codes not covered here are covered elsewhere: E1xxx/E2001/E2002 by
`tests/fixtures/manifest/` (model unit layer), E3003 by this matrix's
`bad-merkle-root` (and `bad-digest-field`) plus the `verify`/`incremental`
unit tests, E5001/E5002 by dedicated CLI cases.

E3002 (proof format invalid) is currently **zero coverage** and is
declared here honestly: the CLI does not consume `proofs/` in the MVP,
and no test in the suite fires E3002. It is reserved in the error-code
contract for a future inclusion-proof CLI path. The incremental
verification path was corrected to surface E3003 (root mismatch), not
E3002 — see `src/verify/incremental_wbtest.mbt` (asserts `E3003`,
explicitly notes E3002 is reserved for "proof format invalid").
